g2u01.w.subject.note: Definition 'd' = "Maths, art, music and history are all kinds of these." 'kind' is taught only as the hedge 'kind of' (g1-u14, = sort of / quite); the plural count noun 'kinds of' (= types/categories, Arten) is an untaught meaning, and the cataphoric 'these' pointing forward to the gapped answer is knotty for A1+. Defensible (standing accepted warn): the four fronted examples plus the s-sentence ("English is my favourite ___ of all.") still scaffold the answer. Definition and carrier are byte-identical to round 5; no new issue. Sibling subject defs (science, geography, history, etc.) read cleanly.

g2u01.w.webpage.note: Definition d="You read it on a tablet or mobile phone." states a property, not an identity, so mc distractors "calendar" and "timetable" satisfy it literally (you can read either on a tablet) and the carrier "Open the school ___ and read it on your tablet." admits them too. They are different objects, not true synonyms of "webpage", so per the mc calibration this is warn not fix; it is the known accepted property-style-definition warn. Tighten the definition to a webpage-specific cue to retire it.
